description:
# AOT runtime has no path to learn tuple shapes

## What's wrong

`fz_aot_setup` creates a fresh empty `SchemaRegistry` and never
registers user tuple schemas. The first call to `fz_alloc_struct`
panics with "index out of bounds: len 0 index 0". JIT doesn't hit
this because compiler and runtime share the same in-memory
`SchemaRegistry` ŌĆö both see the schemas the codegen pre-registered.

Latent until now: no current fixture uses tagged tuples in source.
The bitstring AOT fixture (`shared_heap_send_large_bitstring`)
only uses `MakeBitstring`, which doesn't allocate tuples ŌĆö the
tuple-allocating bitstring fns (`fz_bs_read_field` etc.) aren't on
its codepath. Hidden gap waiting for the first tuple-using AOT
program to trip it.

## The fix in plain English

At build time the compiler knows every tuple arity the program will
need (`tuple_arities` set in `compile_with_backend`). Bake that list
into a `.data` section of the object file. At startup, the AOT
runtime reads the list and registers a `Tuple{N}` schema for each
arity in order. Because both the compiler and the runtime walk the
arities in sorted order, the schema ids they produce match ŌĆö so the
`iconst schema_id` values the compiler baked into the CLIF resolve
correctly at runtime.

## Atomic steps

1. **Lift** the `Tuple{N}` Schema builder out of `ir_codegen.rs`
   into `runtime/heap.rs` as `Schema::tuple_of_arity(n)`. Single
   source of truth used by both codegen and the new runtime path.

2. **Sort** `tuple_arities` iteration at codegen
   (`ir_codegen.rs:1786`): change the set from `HashSet` to
   `BTreeSet` (or sort-then-iterate). Schema ids become
   deterministic. JIT continues to work unchanged because it
   reads back the ids from the registry; AOT now has a meaningful
   contract with the runtime.

3. **CompiledMetadata** carries a `tuple_arities: Vec<u32>` in
   sorted order. JIT ignores it; AOT consumes it.

4. **AotBackend** emits a `fz_aot_tuple_arities` `.data` symbol
   containing the arity array.

5. **emit_aot_c_main** passes `(arities_ptr, arities_len)` into an
   extended `fz_aot_setup` signature.

6. **fz_aot_setup** (in `runtime/aot_shim.rs`) registers each
   `Tuple{N}` via `Schema::tuple_of_arity` in order, producing
   schema ids 0..N-1 that match what codegen baked into the CLIF.
   When arity 1 or 3 is present, sets
   `process.bs_tuple_arity1_schema` / `bs_tuple_arity3_schema` to
   the new ids ŌĆö bringing AOT to parity with how JIT populates
   these via `CompiledModule`.

7. **Optional unification** (same commit): refactor the JIT path
   (`CompiledModule::run`) to set
   `bs_tuple_arity{1,3}_schema` via the same mechanism so there's
   exactly one path. Removes a JIT-only special case.

## What's removed

- Open-coded `Schema { name: format!("Tuple{}"ŌĆ”), size: ŌĆ”, fields:
  ŌĆ” }` block in `ir_codegen.rs:1786-1796` (lifted to runtime helper).
- Non-determinism in tuple schema id assignment (tightened to
  sorted-iteration invariant).
- JIT-only special wiring of `bs_tuple_arity{1,3}` on Process
  (unified with AOT path).

## Acceptance

- `fn main(), do: print({:ok, 42})` runs cleanly via
  `fz build ŌĆ” && ./a.out` printing `{:ok, 42}`.
- `shared_heap_send_large_bitstring::aot` continues to pass
  (`bs_tuple_arity1/3_schema` still populated, just by the new
  unified path).
- All 48 interp + 48 jit + 13 aot fixture legs still pass.
- Existing golden CLIF unchanged for all current fixtures (no
  fixture today pattern-matches on tuples).

## Out of scope

- Refactoring `Process.bs_tuple_arity{1,3}_schema` Option caches
  away (perf-sensitive bs reader hot path; separate concern).
- The fz-i82 multi-call CPS truncation.
- The `ast_eval` fixture itself ŌĆö fz-ul4.37 follows.
title: AOT: register tuple schemas at startup
